meta.title: The Clever Monkey — Set 3
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Story sequence and character quiz for 'The Clever Monkey.'

questions.question: Who are the main characters in 'The Clever Monkey'?
options: A monkey and a lion
Two cats and a monkey
Two dogs and a monkey
A cat and a bird
questions.explanation: The main characters are two cats and a monkey. The cats found the cheese, and the monkey tricked them.

questions.question: The monkey divided the cheese BEFORE the cats started arguing.
questions.explanation: The cats were already arguing about who should divide the cheese BEFORE the monkey came down from the tree.

questions.question: What happened FIRST in the story?
options: The monkey ate a little cheese
Two cats found a large piece of cheese
The cats argued about tiny pieces
The monkey swung down from a tree
questions.explanation: The story begins with two cats finding a large piece of cheese in the jungle. This is the very first event.

questions.question: What did the monkey do AFTER the cats agreed to let him divide the cheese?
options: He ran away with the cheese
He divided the cheese and gave it back right away
He divided the cheese and said the pieces were not equal
He called his friends to share the cheese
questions.explanation: After dividing the cheese, the monkey said 'Uh-oh, I have made a mistake. The pieces are not equal.' This is how his trick started.

questions.question: The monkey in the story lived in a _______ above the two cats.
questions.explanation: The monkey was sitting in a tree watching the cats. He swung down when he saw a chance to get the cheese.

questions.question: Put the events in order from the story. Arrange the words to describe what the monkey did first:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'He swung down from the tree.' This was the monkey's first action after watching the cats.

questions.question: The monkey was clever because he used the cats' argument to get cheese for himself.
questions.explanation: Yes! The monkey was clever — he saw that the cats were arguing and used this as a chance to eat their cheese piece by piece.

questions.question: What happened JUST BEFORE the monkey said 'Uh-oh, I am afraid I ate too much'?
options: The monkeys ate all the cheese
The monkey ate a little of one piece to make them 'equal'
The cats ate their tiny pieces
The monkey divided the cheese perfectly
questions.explanation: The monkey first ate a bit from one piece. Then he said 'Uh-oh' and claimed he ate too much, so he 'needed' to fix the other piece too.

questions.question: Sort these actions — did the MONKEY do it or the CATS do it?
items: ate the cheese little by little

categories.label: Monkey
answers: ate the cheese little by little

questions.explanation: The cats found the cheese and argued. The monkey divided and ate the cheese bit by bit with his clever trick.

questions.question: Why did the second cat insist that the cheese be divided equally?
options: The second cat wanted the monkey to stop eating
The second cat wanted to make sure both cats got the same amount
The second cat did not like cheese
The second cat wanted to give the cheese to the monkey
questions.explanation: The second cat said 'We need to have the cheese divided equally' because it wanted to make sure both cats got a fair and equal share.

questions.question: At the end, the monkey's _______ got bigger and bigger as the cheese got smaller.
questions.explanation: As the monkey kept eating to 'fix' the pieces, his belly (stomach) got bigger and bigger — he was eating all the cheese!

questions.question: At the very end of the story, the two cats shared their tiny pieces of cheese peacefully.
questions.explanation: At the end, the cats were still arguing! One said 'Your piece is bigger' and the other said 'No, your piece is bigger.' They never stopped arguing.

questions.question: How do you think the cats felt at the end of the story?
options: Happy and full
Sad and foolish
Angry at the monkey and ready to get more cheese
Relieved that the monkey helped them
questions.explanation: The cats 'looked sadly at their two tiny pieces.' They were sad because they had lost almost all their cheese, and were still arguing — they felt foolish.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence about the ending:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The two cats are still arguing.' The story says 'for all we know, they are still there arguing.'

questions.question: What kind of story is 'The Clever Monkey'?
options: A true story about animals in Africa
A folktale that teaches a lesson
A science article about monkeys
A poem about the jungle
questions.explanation: 'The Clever Monkey' is a folktale — a traditional story that is passed down and teaches a lesson (moral). The lesson here is that arguing can cause you to lose everything.
